description: System dependencies required for playground>=0.0.5 pip package
notes: Playground is a Google DeepMind package for reinforcement learning and robotics simulation environments, built on top of MuJoCo, Brax, JAX, and Flax. System dependencies are required for several key components: (1) MuJoCo physics engine requires OpenGL libraries for rendering (libgl1-mesa-dev, libgl1-mesa-glx, libglew-dev for OpenGL extensions, libosmesa6-dev for headless/off-screen rendering, libglfw3/libglfw3-dev for windowing), (2) JAX/Flax numerical computing requires standard build tools and optimized linear algebra libraries (build-essential, gcc, g++, gfortran, libopenblas-dev, liblapack-dev, libblas-dev) for compiling native extensions and runtime performance, (3) lxml XML parsing requires libxml2-dev and libxslt1-dev for processing XML/XSLT, (4) various dependencies need zlib for compression support, and (5) patchelf is required on Linux for post-installation binary patching. On macOS with Homebrew, OpenGL is system-provided, so only GLEW, GLFW, and the other libraries are needed. On NixOS, libGL, libGLU, and mesa provide OpenGL support with namespaced packages. The package requires Python 3.9+ and ships with pre-compiled binaries for most platforms via PyPI wheels, but system libraries are needed for runtime linking. For GPU acceleration, JAX requires CUDA/cuDNN libraries (not listed here). Playground is primarily used in research and development for training RL agents in simulated physics environments.
